task_completion.visual_evidence: The VeraCrypt graphical user interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Select slot 1 in the VeraCrypt volume list
task_completion.visual_evidence: Slot 1 (the first row in the drive list) is highlighted or selected in the VeraCrypt main window.

task_completion.description: Navigate to and select the target volume file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The volume path text box in the VeraCrypt main window displays '/home/ga/Volumes/data_volume.hc', or a file selection dialog is visible actively selecting this specific file.

task_completion.description: Click 'Mount' and trigger the password prompt
task_completion.visual_evidence: A VeraCrypt password entry dialog is visible on the screen, indicating the 'Mount' button was clicked.
The VeraCrypt main window shows slot 1 populated with volume details (e.g., Size, Encryption Algorithm) and the Mount Directory column shows '/media/veracrypt1' (or a similar valid mount point).

integrity.description: The agent used the GUI to mount the volume, not terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing command-line VeraCrypt instructions (e.g., 'veracrypt -t' or 'veracrypt --mount') used to bypass the GUI requirement.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual VeraCrypt application
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots must show the genuine VeraCrypt GUI window, not a text editor or a mocked interface.

integrity.description: Password was entered via the GUI prompt
integrity.visual_evidence: The password dialog in the GUI must be used. The password 'MountMe2024' should not be visible as a plain-text command-line argument in a terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target volume: /home/ga/Volumes/data_volume.hc. Password: MountMe2024. Expected mount point: /media/veracrypt1. Expected files inside the volume (if the agent opens the file manager to verify): SF312_Nondisclosure_Agreement.txt, FY2024_Revenue_Budget.csv, backup_authorized_keys.
